questions.question: Which operational milestone in May 2025 served as the catalyst for the accelerated transition of the Indian Armed Forces into formal Integrated Theatre Commands?
questions.explanation: Operation Sindoor (May 2025) was a landmark tri-service operation that successfully validated 'jointness' in combat. It exposed critical gaps in air-land integration, leading to the 2026 budgetary push for formal Theatre Command structures.

questions.question: The Union Budget 2026-27 raised the defence outlay to approximately what percentage of India's GDP for the first time in recent years?
questions.explanation: The 2026-27 Union Budget made a historic leap by allocating ₹7.85 lakh crore to the Ministry of Defence, aiming to touch the 2% GDP benchmark to support aggressive modernization.

questions.question: Which private Indian space-tech startup achieved a milestone in 2025 by conducting the successful Stage-1 static test of its 'Vikram-1' rocket?
questions.explanation: Skyroot Aerospace conducted the successful Stage-1 static test of Vikram-1 in August 2025, paving the way for India's first orbital launch by a private player.

questions.question: In January 2026, India signed a 'Landmark Security Pact' with which global entity, expanding cooperation into the new frontiers of cyber, space, and maritime security?
questions.explanation: India and the EU signed a comprehensive security pact in early 2026, marking a shift from purely economic engagement to a strategic military and technology partnership.

questions.question: Who became the first Indian astronaut to travel to the International Space Station (ISS) aboard the Axiom-4 mission in 2025?
questions.explanation: Gp Capt Shubhanshu Shukla flew to the ISS in 2025 as part of the Axiom-4 mission, serving as a critical precursor to ISRO's indigenous crewed Gaganyaan mission.

questions.question: The third Arihant-class nuclear-powered ballistic missile submarine (SSBN) commissioned in early 2026 is named:
questions.explanation: INS Aridaman, the third SSBN in the Arihant class, was commissioned in early 2026, significantly strengthening India's sea-based nuclear second-strike capability.

questions.question: In late 2025, IN-SPACe operationalized a dedicated Venture Capital Fund for the Indian space sector. What is the total corpus of this fund?
questions.explanation: The ₹1,000 Crore Venture Capital Fund was launched by IN-SPACe and SIDBI in November 2025 to fuel growth in the indigenous private space ecosystem.

questions.question: The 'India-Republic of Korea Strategic Vision 2026–2030' primarily targets collaboration in which high-tech sector known as 'Pax Silica'?
questions.explanation: The 2026 Strategic Vision document with South Korea focuses on semiconductors ('Pax Silica'), shipbuilding, and critical minerals to diversify from global supply chain dependencies.

questions.question: The 'SIGHT Scheme', frequently in the news during 2025-26, is a sub-component of which national mission?
questions.explanation: The Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) scheme provides financial incentives for the domestic manufacturing of electrolyzers and production of Green Hydrogen.

questions.question: As of early 2026, the Indian Air Force has successfully operationalized three squadrons of the S-400 Triumf system. Under what indigenous name are these units designated?
questions.explanation: The S-400 missile squadrons have been officially renamed as 'Sudarshan' units by the Indian Air Force as they were operationalized between 2024 and 2026.

questions.question: Who was the only recipient of the 'Ashok Chakra', India's highest peacetime gallantry award, on Republic Day 2026?
questions.explanation: Group Captain Shubhanshu Shukla was awarded the Ashok Chakra in January 2026. This recognition followed his historic role in the Axiom-4 mission to the ISS and his leadership in the Gaganyaan pilot training program.

questions.question: Which new specialized infrastructure corridors were announced in the Union Budget 2026-27 to support the 'Strategic Resilience' of India's mineral security?
questions.explanation: The 2026-27 Budget announced 'Dedicated Rare Earth Corridors' in Odisha, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, and Tamil Nadu to streamline the supply chain for critical minerals used in high-tech defence and electronics.

questions.question: According to the ISRO roadmap updated in early 2026, which mission is scheduled for launch in late 2026 to study the Martian atmosphere and potential for past life?
questions.explanation: Mangalyaan-2 (Mars Orbiter Mission 2) is slated for a late 2026 launch. Unlike its predecessor, it will carry a more sophisticated payload suite to study the red planet's surface and atmosphere in higher resolution.

questions.question: The 17th BRICS Summit (July 2025) was held in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. Which significant outcome regarding regional integration was achieved there?

options.C: Launch of the BRICS Space Station
options.D: Establishment of a common BRICS currency

questions.explanation: The 2025 Brazil summit marked the operational rollout of 'BRICS Pay', a blockchain-based multi-currency payment system aimed at de-dollarization among member states.

questions.question: At the 47th ASEAN Summit held in October 2025 in Kuala Lumpur, which nation was formally admitted as the 11th full member of the bloc?
questions.explanation: After years as an observer, Timor-Leste was formally admitted as the 11th member of ASEAN during the 2025 summit in Malaysia.

questions.question: Which country did the Indian Navy assist under 'Operation Sagar Bandhu' in 2025, providing cyclone relief and humanitarian aid?
questions.explanation: Operation Sagar Bandhu was launched by the Indian Navy in 2025 to provide immediate relief and medical aid to Sri Lanka following a devastating tropical cyclone.

questions.question: Who was awarded the 'Indira Gandhi Prize for Peace, Disarmament and Development' for the year 2025?
questions.explanation: Michelle Bachelet, the former President of Chile and UN High Commissioner for Human Rights, was conferred the 2025 Indira Gandhi Peace Prize for her work in gender equality and human rights.

questions.question: As per the Economic Survey 2025-26, India's Foreign Exchange Reserves touched which milestone mark in January 2026?
questions.explanation: India's forex reserves hit a historic USD 701.4 billion as of mid-January 2026, providing an import cover of approximately 11 months.

questions.question: In early 2026, India placed the 'Indus Waters Treaty' (IWT) in abeyance. What was the cited strategic reason for this move?

options.A: Technical disputes over the Kishanganga project
options.B: Pakistan's continued support for cross-border terrorism
options.C: Failure of the World Bank to mediate
options.D: Withdrawal of Pakistan from the Permanent Indus Commission

questions.explanation: India suspended the IWT in 2025-26 (placed in abeyance), iterating that the treaty cannot function in an environment of cross-border terrorism, following the 2025 Pahalgam attack.

questions.question: Which city hosted the 'Bharat Bodhan AI Conclave 2026' focusing on creating a sovereign AI ecosystem for Indian education?
questions.explanation: Organized by the Ministry of Education in early 2026 in New Delhi, the conclave focused on integrating Large Language Models (LLMs) into the Indian curriculum.

questions.question: Which domestic tri-service exercise was conducted at Air Force Station Jodhpur in February 2026 to refine integrated electronic warfare and desert combat capabilities?
questions.explanation: Exercise Desert Hunt (Feb 2026) was a major domestic drill involving the Army, Navy, and Air Force to test jointness in the desert sector, with a specific focus on drone integration and cyber-physical systems.

questions.question: As per the Union Budget 2026-27, which scheme has officially replaced the MGNREGA with an increased focus on asset creation and digital wage transparency?
questions.explanation: The 2026 Budget introduced 'VB-GRAM' (Viksit Bharat - Gramin Rojgar and Asset Management) as the successor to MGNREGA, featuring a 42.8% increase in allocation and mandatory blockchain-based wage payments.

questions.question: On January 27, 2026, India achieved a historic diplomatic milestone by concluding a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) with which major trade bloc?
questions.explanation: After years of negotiation, the India-EU FTA was formally concluded in January 2026. This agreement is expected to significantly boost India’s textile, leather, and machinery exports while providing European firms access to the Indian services market.

questions.question: ISRO commenced its 2026 launch calendar on January 12 with the successful deployment of which primary satellite via the PSLV-C65 mission?
questions.explanation: The EOS-N1 (Earth Observation Satellite - NextGen 1) was launched in early 2026. It features high-revisit capability and was primarily launched for strategic surveillance and disaster monitoring.

questions.question: In 2025, which team clinched the first-ever Women's T20 World Cup for the Blind, hosted in India?
questions.explanation: India’s women’s blind cricket team made history in 2025 by winning the inaugural T20 World Cup for the Blind, defeating Australia in the final held in Bengaluru.

questions.question: The 'Semiconductor Mission 2.0', launched in 2026, specifically focuses on which segment to move India beyond assembly and testing?

options.B: Indigenous Chip Design and Fabless startups

questions.explanation: While version 1.0 focused on setting up Fabs, Semiconductor Mission 2.0 (2026) prioritizes Chip Design and supporting the 'fabless' ecosystem through the Design Linked Incentive (DLI) scheme.

questions.question: In 2025, India launched 'Operation Brahma' to provide emergency assistance to which neighbor following a massive 7.2 magnitude earthquake?
questions.explanation: Operation Brahma was the 2025 HADR mission where India deployed C-17 Globemasters and mobile hospitals to assist Myanmar after a devastating earthquake in its northern Sagaing region.

questions.question: The 'India-New Zealand Free Trade Agreement' factsheet, released in April 2026, highlights the elimination of tariffs on which primary Indian export category?
questions.explanation: The India-NZ FTA (2026) successfully secured zero-tariff access for Indian pharmaceuticals and gems/jewelry, while India provided limited concessions in the dairy sector.

questions.question: The Union Budget 2026-27 has proposed the establishment of 'City Economic Regions' (CERs). How many such regions are initially targeted for development?
questions.explanation: The budget proposed 12 City Economic Regions (CERs) to be mapped as growth drivers, with an allocation of ₹5,000 crore per CER over a five-year period to modernize urban infrastructure.

questions.question: Which critical technology, tested in the PSLV-C63 (TDS-01) mission in late 2025, allows satellites to reduce fuel weight from 2 tonnes to approximately 200 kg?
questions.explanation: The High Thrust Electric Propulsion System (tested in 2025-26) uses electric power to accelerate ions, providing high efficiency that allows for much lighter satellites and longer mission life.

questions.question: The Very Short-Range Air Defence System (VSHORADS), successfully flight-tested by DRDO in February 2025, is primarily designed to neutralize which type of aerial threats?

options.B: Low-altitude aerial threats at short ranges

questions.explanation: VSHORADS is a Man-Portable Air Defence System (MANPAD) designed by DRDO to neutralize low-altitude aerial threats at short ranges, essential for decentralized air defence in rugged terrain.

questions.question: Who was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 2025 for her efforts in promoting democratic transitions and human rights in South America?
questions.explanation: Maria Corina Machado of Venezuela was awarded the 2025 Nobel Peace Prize for her relentless pursuit of democratic restoration and civil liberties amidst political crisis.

questions.question: Which subsidiary of ISRO reported a revenue of approximately ₹800 crore in FY25, driven by global commercial launch services and transponder leasing?
questions.explanation: NSIL is the commercial arm of ISRO. In FY25, it achieved significant growth by executing over 15 commercial launches and expanding its satellite manufacturing contracts for international clients.

questions.question: According to the Economic Survey 2025-26, what was the average headline Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation recorded during April–December 2025?
questions.explanation: The 2025-26 Economic Survey highlighted a sharp decline in inflation to 1.7%, the lowest ever, primarily driven by disinflation in food and fuel sectors.

questions.question: In August 2025, the Indian Government approved the purchase of how many additional Tejas Mk1A fighter jets to bolster the IAF's squadron strength?
questions.explanation: The DAC cleared the acquisition of 97 more Tejas Mk1A fighters in 2025 in a deal worth approximately $7.4 billion, reinforcing the indigenous push in defence aviation.

questions.question: Who was named 'Player of the Match' in the 2026 ICC Men's T20 World Cup final where India defeated New Zealand by 96 runs?
questions.explanation: Jasprit Bumrah's exceptional spell of 4 wickets for 15 runs in the March 2026 final at the Narendra Modi Stadium earned him the Player of the Match title.

questions.question: The 'New Delhi Declaration' adopted at the 2026 AI Impact Summit focused on which of the following multilateral priorities?

options.A: A ban on all autonomous weapon systems
options.B: Democratisation of AI and 'safe and trusted' AI systems
options.D: Establishing a global AI police force

questions.explanation: The 2026 summit emphasised that AI development should be inclusive and secure, focusing on 'Safe and Trusted AI' as a global public good.

questions.question: Which rocket mission in January 2025 served as the foundational test for the upcoming 'Bharatiya Antariksh Station'?
questions.explanation: The GSLV-F15 mission successfully tested docking and orbital station-keeping technologies required for India's future space station modular assembly.

questions.question: Under the tech-driven rural initiative 'SVAMITVA', how many property cards had been issued by the end of 2025 to enable rural citizens to use property as a financial asset?
questions.explanation: SVAMITVA uses drone technology for village mapping. By late 2025, 2.76 crore property cards were issued, covering over 3.28 lakh villages.

questions.question: India's rank in the Global Innovation Index (GII) improved from 81 in 2015 to which position in 2025?
questions.explanation: Reflecting a decade of consistent digital and scientific progress, India reached the 38th spot in 2025, bolstered by a surge in technology startups and patent filings.

questions.question: On May 8, 2026, DRDO successfully flight-tested an advanced Agni missile with MIRV technology. What is the primary tactical advantage of a MIRV-capable system like the Agni-V?

options.A: It allows a single missile to carry multiple warheads to different targets independently.
options.B: It enables the missile to be launched from a submarine under ice.
options.C: It provides a purely conventional (non-nuclear) supersonic strike capability.
options.D: It uses AI to change its destination mid-flight based on satellite feedback.

questions.explanation: Multiple Independently Targetable Re-entry Vehicle (MIRV) technology allows one missile to deliver several warheads, each hitting a distinct target. This significantly enhances second-strike capability and can overwhelm enemy missile defense systems.

questions.question: The Union Budget 2026-27 introduced the 'Biopharma SHAKTI' mission. What is the primary financial corpus and objective of this mission?

options.A: ₹5,000 crore; To promote indigenous Ayurvedic medicine exports.
options.B: ₹10,000 crore; To make India a global hub for biologics and biosimilars.
options.C: ₹15,000 crore; To provide free vaccines to all SAARC nations.
options.D: ₹20,000 crore; To build specialized pharma-specific cold chain logistics.

questions.explanation: Biopharma SHAKTI is a ₹10,000 crore mission aimed at strengthening India's position in the high-value biologics and biosimilars market, focusing on research and regulatory capacity building.

questions.question: In early 2026, India announced that the year would be celebrated as the 'ASEAN-India Year of ______' to enhance strategic depth in the Indo-Pacific.
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Modi declared 2026 as the 'ASEAN-India Year of Maritime Cooperation' during the ASEAN-India summit, focusing on blue economy, security, and freedom of navigation.

questions.question: Which mission in 2026 is slated to be India's first indigenous crewed spaceflight, aiming to make India the fourth nation to independently send humans to space?
questions.explanation: While Gaganyaan-1, 2, and 3 are uncrewed test flights (including those with the Vyommitra humanoid), Gaganyaan-4 is the first crewed mission scheduled for 2026.

questions.question: According to the 2026-27 Budget Estimates, what is the government's target for the Debt-to-GDP ratio by the end of the fiscal year?
questions.explanation: The government estimates the Debt-to-GDP ratio to decline to 55.6% in 2026-27, with a long-term goal of reaching 50% by 2030-31 to ensure fiscal sustainability.

questions.question: Which team won its maiden Ranji Trophy title in the 2025–26 season, defeating Punjab in the final?
questions.explanation: Jammu & Kashmir made history by winning their first-ever Ranji Trophy title in the 2025-26 season, showcasing the growing cricketing talent in the region.

questions.question: As of May 2026, who holds the position of Chief of Defence Staff (CDS) of India, having previously served as the Vice Chief of the Army Staff?
questions.explanation: General Upendra Dwivedi is the current CDS as of May 2026. He previously served as the Vice Chief of the Army Staff and the General Officer Commanding-in-Chief of the Northern Command.

questions.question: The 'Pradhan Mantri Janjatiya Unnat Gram Abhiyan', launched in 2024 with a mission to cover 63,000 villages, is scheduled for completion in which year?
questions.explanation: The scheme aims for the comprehensive development of tribal-majority villages and is targeted for completion of its primary infrastructure goals by late 2026.

questions.question: The 'Pradhan Mantri Surya Ghar Yojana' (2024) aims to solarize how many households by 2027 to reduce carbon emissions and electricity bills?
questions.explanation: This scheme targets 1 crore households for rooftop solar installations, providing 300 units of free electricity and contributing to India's Net Zero goals.

questions.question: The IMF in its April 2026 report projected India's GDP growth rate for 2026 and 2027 at ______%, the highest among major economies.
questions.explanation: The IMF projected a steady 6.5% growth for India in 2026 and 2027, highlighting resilience despite global energy price volatility and geopolitical risks.

questions.question: Which multi-national naval exercise hosted by India in February 2026 saw participation from the highest number of countries in its history (over 70 nations)?
questions.explanation: MILAN 2026, held at Visakhapatnam, became the largest edition of the biennial exercise, emphasizing India's role as a 'Preferred Security Partner' in the IOR.

questions.question: In May 2026, ISRO successfully conducted the 'SpaDeX' experiment. What was the primary technology being validated in this mission?

options.B: Autonomous docking and berthing of spacecraft.

questions.explanation: SpaDeX (Space Docking Experiment) is critical for India's plans to build the 'Bharatiya Antariksh Station' and for sample-return missions like Chandrayaan-4.

questions.question: The 'Coastal Cargo Promotion Scheme' mentioned in the 2026 Budget aims to double the share of waterways in India's modal mix from 6% to ______% by 2047.
questions.explanation: The scheme provides incentives to shift freight from road/rail to waterways, targeting 12% share by 2047 as part of the 'Amrit Kaal' infrastructure goals.

questions.question: Which country won the 2026 World Baseball Classic, defeating the USA in the final?
questions.explanation: Venezuela clinched its first-ever World Baseball Classic title in 2026, with Bryce Harper's crucial home run in the final being a major highlight.

questions.question: In early 2026, Sri Lanka launched its 'One Health' drive specifically to combat which rising global health threat?
questions.explanation: The 'One Health' initiative integrates human, animal, and environmental health to manage the spread of drug-resistant infections (AMR), supported by WHO.

questions.question: The second batch of 24 MH-60R 'Romeo' Seahawk helicopters, fully integrated with Indian Navy weapons like the 'Advanced Light Torpedo', was operationalized in ______.
questions.explanation: The integration was completed in early 2026, enhancing the Navy's Anti-Submarine Warfare (ASW) and Search and Rescue (SAR) capabilities.

questions.question: What are the 'Three Kartavyas' outlined by the Finance Minister in the Union Budget 2026–27 to drive India towards Viksit Bharat @ 2047?

options.B: Sustain Growth, Build Capacity, Sabka Vikas.

questions.explanation: The 2026-27 Budget pillars are: 1. Accelerate and Sustain Economic Growth, 2. Fulfill Aspirations and Build Capacity, 3. Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas (Inclusion).

questions.question: Chandrayaan-4, planned for a launch late in 2026 or early 2027, differs from Chandrayaan-3 primarily because:

options.A: It will land on the lunar North Pole.
options.B: It is a dedicated lunar sample-return mission.
options.C: It is a crewed mission with two astronauts.
options.D: It will only orbit the Moon to map water ice.

questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-4 is a complex mission designed to land on the Moon, collect samples, and return them to Earth, involving multiple docking maneuvers.

questions.question: Sumit Antil won the Gold medal in which event at the World Para Athletics Grand Prix in 2026?
questions.explanation: Sumit Antil, a double Paralympic gold medalist, continued his dominance in Javelin Throw (F64 category) at the 2026 Grand Prix.

questions.question: In May 2026, India signed a major agreement with ______ to foster joint research in clean energy technologies like 'Smart Grids' and 'Energy Storage'.
questions.explanation: The Partnership to Advance Clean Energy Research (PACE-R) focuses on solar, biofuels, and grid efficiency, strengthening the India-US tech partnership.

questions.question: On May 08, 2026, India successfully flight-tested an advanced Agni missile from Dr APJ Abdul Kalam Island. Which specific advanced capability was validated during this test?
questions.explanation: The May 2026 test confirmed the MIRV capability, allowing a single Agni missile to deliver multiple payloads to different targets across a large area, significantly enhancing India’s strategic deterrence.

questions.question: Which nuclear-powered ballistic missile submarine (SSBN), the third of its class, was commissioned into the Indian Navy in April 2026?
questions.explanation: INS Aridaman was commissioned in April 2026, strengthening the sea-based leg of India’s nuclear triad following the induction of INS Arihant and INS Arighat.

questions.question: In the Union Budget 2026-27, what is the approximately total allocation for the Ministry of Defence, marking a significant rise to support the shift toward tech-centric warfare?
questions.explanation: The 2026-27 Budget saw a record ₹7.85 lakh crore allocation for Defence, reflecting a push for AI integration, drone warfare, and indigenous manufacturing.

questions.question: Who was appointed as the Chief of Defence Staff (CDS) of India in May 2026, succeeding General Anil Chauhan?
questions.explanation: Lt Gen NS Raja Subramani was appointed as the new CDS in May 2026, tasked with overseeing the transition of the Indian Armed Forces into Integrated Theatre Commands.

questions.question: During PM Modi’s visit to the UAE in May 2026, the focus of the Strategic Defence Partnership shifted from a buyer-seller relationship to which mechanism?
questions.explanation: The visit emphasized the JDCC as the primary institutional mechanism for co-development and co-production of military hardware between India and the UAE.

questions.question: Which new ship in the 'Adamya-class' Fast Patrol Vessel series was commissioned into the Indian Coast Guard in early May 2026?
questions.explanation: ICGS Achal was commissioned in May 2026, specifically designed for coastal surveillance, search and rescue, and anti-smuggling operations with over 50% indigenous content.

questions.question: In May 2026, INS Sagardhwani visited which country to strengthen maritime and scientific collaboration, specifically focusing on oceanographic research?
questions.explanation: INS Sagardhwani’s visit to Cam Ranh, Vietnam (May 4-8, 2026) was a key step in expanding bilateral scientific and training initiatives in the South China Sea region.

questions.question: What is India's rank in the World Press Freedom Index 2026, as released by Reporters Without Borders in May 2026?
questions.explanation: In the 2026 index, India was ranked 159th out of 180 countries, indicating a slight shift in the evaluation of the press environment globally.

questions.question: The Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) launched which AI-based platform in April 2026 to streamline scientific research applications?
questions.explanation: SARAL AI (Scientific Analysis and Research Assistance Link) was developed by ANRF in 2026 to assist researchers in data analysis and grant management.

questions.question: India's defence exports reached an unprecedented milestone of what amount in the financial year 2025-26?
questions.explanation: Defence exports hit a record ₹38,424 crore in FY 2025-26, growing by over 60% year-on-year, driven by demand for BrahMos, Pinaka, and Tejas platforms.

questions.question: The 'Desert Knight 2025' air exercise featured the participation of India alongside which two nations in the Arabian Sea?
questions.explanation: Exercise Desert Knight (conducted late 2024 into early 2025) involved the IAF alongside the French Air and Space Force and the UAE Air Force to enhance interoperability in the IOR.

questions.question: The 'NISAR' satellite, scheduled for its final orbital positioning in 2026, is a collaboration between ISRO and which international space agency?
questions.explanation: NISAR (NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar) is a dual-frequency SAR satellite designed for global Earth observation and monitoring of climate change.

questions.question: India announced its 'Green Hydrogen Hub' status for which two specific ports during the 2026 Maritime Summit?
questions.explanation: V.O. Chidambaranar Port (Tuticorin) and Deendayal Port (Kandla) were designated as the first major Green Hydrogen Hubs in 2026 to support the National Green Hydrogen Mission.

questions.question: Which nation assumed the G20 Presidency for the year 2026, succeeding South Africa?
questions.explanation: The United States assumed the G20 Presidency for 2026, marking a critical year for global economic coordination following South Africa’s 2025 term.

questions.question: What is 'TARA', which was in news in May 2026 regarding DRDO’s advancements in aerial warfare?
questions.explanation: TARA is an indigenous stand-off glide bomb developed by DRDO, successfully tested in early May 2026 to enable high-precision strikes from long ranges.

questions.question: The 'Digital India Act 2026' was tabled in Parliament to replace which century-old legislation?

options.C: Press and Registration of Books Act

questions.explanation: The Digital India Act 2026 aims to provide a modern legal framework for the internet, focusing on online safety and trust, replacing the outdated IT Act 2000.

questions.question: The 2026 Winter Olympics, also known as Milano Cortina 2026, are scheduled to be held in which country?
questions.explanation: Milano Cortina 2026 will be hosted by Italy, with events spread across Milan and Cortina d'Ampezzo in early 2026.

questions.question: Who was awarded the Pulitzer Prize for Fiction in May 2026 for the novel 'The Echo of Silence'?
questions.explanation: Jhumpa Lahiri was awarded the Pulitzer Prize for Fiction in 2026, marking a significant return to themes of identity and migration in her latest work.

questions.question: Which country did India launch 'Operation Karuna' in 2024 to assist following the impact of Cyclone Mocha?
questions.explanation: India launched Operation Karuna in May 2024, deploying multiple naval ships carrying emergency food, medicines, and water to help Myanmar after the cyclone.

questions.question: In the April 2026 Monetary Policy Review, the RBI maintained the Repo Rate at what level to balance growth and inflation targets?
questions.explanation: The RBI continued with the 6.50% repo rate in early 2026 to ensure inflation stayed within the 4% target while supporting a resilient GDP growth projection.

questions.question: In May 2026, the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) and the IAF successfully flight-tested 'TARA'. What is the primary classification of this system?
questions.explanation: TARA (Tactical Airborne Range Augmentation) is India's first indigenous long-range glide weapon. It was successfully tested in early May 2026, enhancing the IAF's stand-off strike capabilities.

questions.question: Which frigate, the sixth ship of the Nilgiri-class (Project 17A), was officially inducted into the Indian Navy in May 2026?
questions.explanation: INS Mahendragiri, built by Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited (MDL), was delivered and inducted in May 2026. It features advanced stealth features and indigenous weapons systems.

questions.question: India hosted the 'India AI Impact Summit' in February 2026. Which city was the primary venue for this global event focusing on the ethics and governance of AI?
questions.explanation: The India AI Impact Summit 2026 was held in New Delhi, bringing together global leaders and tech giants to discuss the 'New Delhi Declaration' on safe and trusted AI.

questions.question: Which critical orbital milestone did ISRO achieve on January 16, 2025, which is foundational for the future 'Bharatiya Antariksh Station'?
questions.explanation: ISRO successfully conducted the SpaDeX (Space Docking Experiment) in early 2025, making India the fourth country to master satellite docking technology in orbit.

questions.question: In early 2026, the Indian Navy operationalized its first squadron of MH-60R 'Romeo' helicopters. Which country is the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) for these platforms?
questions.explanation: The MH-60R multi-role helicopters, manufactured by Lockheed Martin (USA), were operationalized in early 2026 to enhance the Navy's anti-submarine and maritime search-and-rescue capabilities.

questions.question: In the Union Budget 2026-27, which sector received a record-high allocation of ₹7.85 lakh crore?
questions.explanation: Defence continues to lead the sectoral allocations in the 2026-27 Budget, with ₹7.85 lakh crore focused on modernization and the 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiative in defence production.

questions.question: The bilateral exercise 'DUSTLIK-VI' was conducted in April 2025. Which nation is India's partner in this counter-terrorism exercise?
questions.explanation: Exercise DUSTLIK is a bilateral exercise between the Indian Army and the Uzbekistan Army, focusing on counter-terrorism operations in semi-urban terrain.

questions.question: The 'Pradhan Mantri Surya Ghar Yojana', frequently in news in 2025-26, aims to provide free electricity to how many households via rooftop solar?
questions.explanation: Launched in early 2024 and expanded in 2025-26, the scheme targets 1 crore households for rooftop solar installations to provide up to 300 units of free electricity per month.

questions.question: Who was named the ICC Men's Player of the Month for August 2025, following a stellar performance in the home series?
questions.explanation: Shubman Gill was awarded the ICC Player of the Month for August 2025, reflecting his dominant form in both domestic and international circuits during that period.

questions.question: Which mission was launched by the Indian Navy in April-May 2025 to evacuate Indian nationals and foreign citizens from a four-day conflict in Pakistan?
questions.explanation: Operation Sindoor (May 2025) was a landmark tri-service operation involving maritime evacuations and strategic deployments during a high-tension period in the region.

questions.question: Which indigenous Large Language Model (LLM), optimized for Indian languages, was highlighted as a major milestone during the 2026 AI Impact Summit?
questions.explanation: Sarvam AI was showcased in early 2026 as a leading indigenous platform, providing multilingual support and ensuring data sovereignty for Indian governance and research.

questions.question: The 'NISAR' mission, a joint Earth-observation collaboration, is scheduled for a final orbital launch in 2026. Which two frequencies does its radar operate on?
questions.explanation: NISAR (NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar) uses L-band and S-band frequencies to map the Earth's surface deformation and ecosystem changes with high precision.

questions.question: In early 2026, which country experienced 'Gen Z protests' that led to a significant diplomatic shift in India's neighborhood policy?
questions.explanation: Nepal witnessed large-scale youth-led 'Gen Z protests' in early 2026, impacting its internal governance and requiring India to recalibrate its regional stability strategy.

questions.question: The S-400 Triumf air defence system, being fast-tracked for full induction in 2026, is officially designated by the IAF as:
questions.explanation: The IAF has designated the Russian-origin S-400 system as 'Sudarshan', symbolizing its role as a protective disc in the nation's multi-layered air defence network.

questions.question: By March 2026, the RBI reached a milestone of processing how many daily transactions using the 'e-Rupee' (Retail CBDC)?
questions.explanation: The Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC-R) hit the 10 million daily transaction mark in early 2026, driven by integration with existing UPI QR codes.

questions.explanation: India was ranked 105th out of 127 countries in 2024, categorized in the 'Serious' hunger level, though the Indian government frequently challenges the report's methodology.

questions.question: At the Paris 2024 Olympics, who became the first Indian athlete to win two medals in a single edition of the Games since Independence?
questions.explanation: Manu Bhaker won two bronze medals in shooting (10m Air Pistol individual and mixed team), making Olympic history for India.

questions.question: The 'MAHASAGAR' initiative, recently promoted by the Indian Navy, stands for:

options.A: Maritime Health and Security Agreement for Global Alliance.
options.B: Maritime Heads for Active Security and Growth for All in the Region.
options.C: Major Hydrographic Assessment and Security along Global Arteries.
options.D: Multilateral Aid and Humanitarian Assistance for South Asian Geopolitics.

questions.explanation: MAHASAGAR is an outreach initiative for high-level interaction between the Indian Navy and heads of maritime agencies from Indian Ocean Region (IOR) littoral states.

questions.question: The 'Sanchar Saathi' portal, which became highly relevant in 2024-25, is primarily used for:

options.B: Blocking lost/stolen mobile phones and checking fraudulent connections.
options.D: Applying for environmental clearances for industrial projects.

questions.explanation: Developed by the Department of Telecommunications, it empowers citizens to secure their mobile connections and report stolen devices.

questions.question: What is the primary range of the 'Pralay' tactical ballistic missile, which the Ministry of Defence approved for deployment along the LAC and LOC in 2024?
questions.explanation: Pralay is a surface-to-surface, short-range ballistic missile (SRBM) developed for battlefield use, specifically to counter high-altitude threats.

questions.question: The 16th BRICS Summit (2024), held in Kazan, Russia, was significant for India as it coincided with:

options.A: India becoming the permanent chair of BRICS.
options.B: A major breakthrough in India-China border disengagement patrolling norms.
options.C: The launch of a common BRICS currency.

questions.explanation: On the sidelines of the Kazan summit, India and China agreed to patrolling arrangements along the LAC, ending the 4-year military standoff in Depsang and Demchok.

questions.question: ISRO’s 'PROBA-3' mission, launched in late 2024, is a collaboration with the European Space Agency (ESA) to study:

options.B: The Solar Corona using formation flying.
options.C: Black holes in the Milky Way.
options.D: Water ice on the South Pole of the Moon.

questions.explanation: PROBA-3 involves two satellites flying in precise formation to create a giant coronagraph to study the Sun's atmosphere.

questions.question: Which class of submarines is being indigenously constructed under the Indian Navy's 'Project 75I'?
questions.explanation: Project 75I (India) aims to build six advanced conventional submarines with AIP technology to allow them to stay submerged for longer periods.

questions.question: The 'India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor' (IMEC) was announced at which summit?
questions.explanation: IMEC is a planned corridor that aims to stimulate economic development through connectivity between Asia, the Arabian Gulf, and Europe.

questions.question: In 2024, India signed a 10-year contract to operate the Shahid Beheshti terminal at which strategic port?
questions.explanation: The long-term agreement for Chabahar Port is vital for India’s connectivity to Central Asia and the International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC), bypassing Pakistan.

questions.question: Exercise 'Tarang Shakti' (2024) was significant for the Indian Air Force as it was:

options.A: The first ever multi-national air exercise hosted by India.
options.B: A secret drill conducted inside the Tibetan plateau.
options.C: A simulation of nuclear war with a neighboring state.
options.D: A test of only women-led fighter squadrons.

questions.explanation: Tarang Shakti involved nearly 30 countries and was the largest international air exercise ever hosted on Indian soil to enhance interoperability.

questions.question: The 'Global Biofuel Alliance' (GBA), launched by India, aims to primarily promote the use of which generation of biofuels to avoid food security issues?
questions.explanation: GBA focuses on non-food feedstock like crop residues (2G) to produce ethanol and other biofuels, aligning with India's 20% ethanol blending target.

questions.question: Which ISRO mission launched in January 2024 is India's first dedicated polarimetry mission to study the dynamics of bright astronomical X-ray sources?
questions.explanation: X-ray Polarimeter Satellite (XPoSat) is only the second such mission in the world after NASA's IXPE, marking a leap in India's space science capabilities.

questions.question: In the 'Global Innovation Index 2024', India's rank improved to which position?
questions.explanation: India moved up to the 39th rank out of 133 economies in the GII 2024, continuing its decade-long climb in innovation performance.

questions.question: India successfully test-fired 'Agni-Prime' (Agni-P) in April 2024. Which of the following is a characteristic of this missile?

options.A: It is a liquid-fueled single-stage missile.
options.B: It is a canisterized, two-stage solid propellant missile.
options.C: It has a range exceeding 10,000 km.
options.D: It is an anti-tank guided missile.

questions.explanation: Agni-Prime is a new generation nuclear-capable ballistic missile with a range of 1000–2000 km, designed for enhanced mobility and quick launch.

questions.question: In 2024, which country officially withdrew from the 'Open Skies Treaty', citing the lack of participation by the United States and Russia?
questions.explanation: Belarus formally suspended the treaty in 2024, following the earlier withdrawal of major powers, effectively ending the aerial surveillance pact intended to build trust.

questions.question: India won the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2024 by defeating which team in the final?
questions.explanation: India defeated South Africa by 7 runs in a thrilling final held at Bridgetown, Barbados, ending an 11-year drought for an ICC trophy.

questions.question: The 'Vadhvan Port' project, which received Union Cabinet approval in June 2024, is set to be one of the top 10 ports in the world. In which state is it located?
questions.explanation: Vadhvan Port (Palghar, Maharashtra) will be an all-weather, deep-water mega port with a natural draft of 20 meters, vital for IMEC connectivity.

questions.question: Which indigenous light combat helicopter (LCH) was officially inducted into the Indian Army and Air Force in 2024-25 and deployed in high-altitude regions?
questions.explanation: Prachand is the only attack helicopter in the world capable of landing and taking off at altitudes as high as 5,000 meters (Siachen Glacier area).

questions.question: The 'CE-20' cryogenic engine, which successfully powered the LVM3 rocket for Chandrayaan-3, was human-rated in 2024 for which mission?
questions.explanation: Human-rating involves rigorous testing to ensure the engine meets safety standards required to carry astronauts in the Gaganyaan mission.

questions.question: The 'Digital Rupee' (CBDC), piloted by RBI, reached a milestone in 2024 by integrating with which existing payment system to increase adoption?
questions.explanation: UPI-CBDC interoperability allows users to scan any UPI QR code and pay using their Digital Rupee wallet, merging convenience with sovereign currency benefits.

questions.question: At COP28 (2024), the 'Loss and Damage Fund' was operationalized. Which country was the first to host the fund's board?
questions.explanation: The Philippines was selected to host the Board of the Loss and Damage Fund, which is designed to help vulnerable nations cope with climate disasters.

questions.question: In 2024, the Indian Army established its first 'Cyber Operations and Support Command' (COSC) to counter threats in which domain?
questions.explanation: COSC is tasked with defending Army networks and conducting operations in the digital/information domain against adversarial influence and cyberattacks.

questions.question: According to the 'SIPRI Report 2024', India remains the world's largest importer of arms. Which country is India’s top arms supplier currently?
questions.explanation: Despite a decrease in percentage share and a rise in French and US imports, Russia remains the largest supplier of weapons to India (accounting for roughly 36%).

questions.question: In May 2024, which island nation’s leadership requested the withdrawal of Indian military personnel stationed to operate aviation platforms?
questions.explanation: Following the election of President Mohamed Muizzu, India replaced its military personnel with civilian technical experts to operate the donated aircraft.

questions.question: The 'BioE3' (Biotechnology for Economy, Environment and Employment) policy, approved by the Cabinet in 2024, aims to promote:

options.B: Mandatory organic farming for all exports.
options.C: Ban on all genetically modified organisms (GMOs).
options.D: Export of traditional medicinal herbs only.

questions.explanation: BioE3 policy focuses on high-performance bio-manufacturing to drive the bio-economy, aiming for a $300 billion bio-economy by 2030.

questions.question: The Indian Navy successfully tested a 'Ship-launched BrahMos' missile with a strike range of approximately how many kilometers in 2024?
questions.explanation: The extended-range BrahMos (ER) has been tested to hit targets beyond the original 290 km limit, significantly increasing the Navy's stand-off strike power.

questions.question: In the Union Budget 2024-25, which specific mission was allocated ₹1,000 crore to catalyze private sector-led space research?

options.A: Venture Capital Fund for Space Sector.

questions.explanation: A ₹1,000 crore VC fund was established to support space-tech startups, emphasizing India's shift toward a commercialized space economy.

questions.question: The 'LVM3-M4' rocket is the chosen launch vehicle for the crewed Gaganyaan mission. What does LVM stand for?
questions.explanation: LVM3 (Launch Vehicle Mark 3) is ISRO's heaviest rocket, previously known as GSLV Mk III, and is now the workhorse for deep space and crewed missions.

questions.question: At the 2024 G7 Summit in Italy, India was invited as an 'Outreach Country'. Which of the following is NOT a member of the G7?
questions.explanation: G7 members are USA, UK, France, Germany, Italy, Canada, and Japan. India is frequently invited as a strategic partner but is not a member.

questions.question: The 'Sela Tunnel', world's longest bi-lane tunnel at an altitude of 13,000 ft, provides all-weather connectivity to which strategic town?
questions.explanation: Located in Arunachal Pradesh, the Sela Tunnel ensures that the Tawang sector remains accessible even during heavy winter snowfall, crucial for troop movement.

questions.question: On Republic Day 2025, several personnel were awarded the 'Kirti Chakra'. What is the rank of this award in the order of peacetime gallantry awards?
questions.explanation: Ashok Chakra is the highest peacetime gallantry award, followed by Kirti Chakra and Shaurya Chakra.

questions.question: India has reached its goal of 40% non-fossil fuel installed electricity capacity well ahead of the 2030 target. What is the new 2030 target for renewable energy capacity?
questions.explanation: India committed at COP26 to reach 500 GW of non-fossil energy capacity by 2030 to combat climate change.

questions.question: Who was appointed as the Chief of the Naval Staff in April 2024, succeeding Admiral R. Hari Kumar?
questions.explanation: Admiral Dinesh K. Tripathi assumed command as the 26th Chief of the Naval Staff.

questions.question: The 'RudraM-II' air-to-surface missile, successfully flight-tested from a Su-30 MKI in May 2024, is primarily designed for:
questions.explanation: RudraM-II is an indigenously developed solid-propelled air-to-surface missile designed to neutralize enemy radar and communication assets from high altitudes.

questions.question: Which nation became the 32nd member of NATO in early 2024, significantly altering the security architecture of the Baltic Sea region?
questions.explanation: Sweden officially joined NATO in March 2024, ending over two centuries of military non-alignment following the geopolitical shift caused by the Russia-Ukraine conflict.

questions.question: In 2024, ISRO's commercial arm NSIL successfully launched the 'GSAT-20' (GSAT-N2) satellite. Which launch provider was used for this mission due to the satellite's high mass?
questions.explanation: Because the GSAT-20 weighed 4,700 kg (exceeding LVM3's capacity), ISRO partnered with SpaceX for a launch via Falcon 9 in late 2024.

questions.question: The Indian Navy commissioned its first squadron of 'MQ-9B SeaGuardian' High Altitude Long Endurance (HALE) drones at which naval base in 2025?
questions.explanation: INS Rajali serves as the premier base for long-range maritime surveillance, hosting both P-8I aircraft and the newly inducted MQ-9B drones.

questions.question: The 'India-UAE Local Currency Settlement System' (LCSS), operationalized in 2024-25, allows bilateral trade to be settled in:
questions.explanation: LCSS aims to reduce transaction costs and dependence on the US Dollar by allowing direct settlement in INR and AED.

questions.question: The 'PM-eBus Sewa' scheme, active in 2024-26, targets the deployment of 10,000 electric buses in how many Indian cities?
questions.explanation: The scheme focuses on cities with a population of 3 lakh and above, including all capital cities of UTs and North Eastern States.

questions.question: In early 2026, India signed the 'Acquisition and Cross-Servicing Agreement' (ACSA) with which European nation to facilitate mutual logistics support?
questions.explanation: The agreement with Germany enhances interoperability during joint exercises and humanitarian assistance missions in the Indo-Pacific.

questions.question: India's newest AI supercomputer 'AIRAWAT' was ranked in the global top 100 in 2024-25. At which institution is it installed?
questions.explanation: AIRAWAT is part of the National Program on AI and is installed at the Center for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC) in Pune.

questions.question: Who was conferred with the 'Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award 2024' for excellence in Badminton?
questions.explanation: The duo was honored for their historic world ranking and winning the Asian Games Gold medal.

questions.question: The 'Drishti 10 Starliner', inducted by the Indian Navy in 2024, is an indigenously manufactured variant of which global UAV?
questions.explanation: Manufactured by Adani Defence and Aerospace in collaboration with Elbit Systems, it is a Medium Altitude Long Endurance (MALE) UAV.

questions.question: Which country hosted the 'Exercise MALABAR 2024', involving the Navies of India, USA, Japan, and Australia?
questions.explanation: The 2024 edition of the QUAD naval exercise was hosted by India off the coast of Visakhapatnam in the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: The 'LUPEX' mission (Lunar Polar Exploration) is a joint mission between ISRO and which other space agency scheduled for 2025-26?
questions.explanation: LUPEX (also known as Chandrayaan-5) is a collaboration with Japan's JAXA to explore the water ice potential at the Moon's South Pole.

questions.question: In 2025, which Indian bank became the first to integrate 'Quantum Key Distribution' (QKD) for securing its inter-branch data communication?
questions.explanation: HDFC Bank successfully piloted QKD technology in 2025 to protect financial data against potential threats from future quantum computers.

questions.question: The 'VL-SRSAM', indigenously developed for the Indian Navy, completed its final development trials in 2024. What does 'VL' stand for?
questions.explanation: Vertical Launch Short Range Surface-to-Air Missile (VL-SRSAM) is designed to neutralize aerial threats including sea-skimming targets.

questions.question: The 'IMEC' corridor, proposed in 2023-24, aims to bypass which significant maritime bottleneck?
questions.explanation: The India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor offers an alternative multi-modal route to Europe, reducing transit time by 40% compared to the Suez Canal route.

questions.question: Which Indian state became the first to implement a 'Carbon Credit Trading Scheme' for its industrial sector in 2025?
questions.explanation: Gujarat launched the pilot to help industries meet emission targets and incentivize renewable energy adoption.

questions.question: Under the 'iCET' framework, India and the USA agreed to joint production of which jet engines in 2024-25?
questions.explanation: General Electric (GE) and HAL signed a deal for the co-production of F414 engines in India for the Tejas Mk2 fighter aircraft.

questions.question: The 2024 Nobel Prize in Physics was awarded to John J. Hopfield and Geoffrey E. Hinton for their foundational discoveries in:

options.B: Machine Learning with Artificial Neural Networks

questions.explanation: Their work provided the building blocks for modern Artificial Intelligence and Deep Learning systems.

questions.question: In 2025, ISRO successfully tested the 'Integrated Main Parachute' for Gaganyaan. How many main parachutes are used to safely land the Crew Module?
questions.explanation: The system uses three main parachutes, although it can safely land with only two in case of a failure (redundancy).

questions.question: The 'Nyoma Airfield', currently being upgraded into a full-fledged fighter base in 2025-26, is located in which strategic region?
questions.explanation: Nyoma is located very close to the Line of Actual Control (LAC) and will become one of the world's highest airfields for fighter operations.

questions.question: In early 2026, the DRDO successfully flight-tested the 'Agni-Prime' missile. Which of the following is a key technological feature that distinguishes it from earlier Agni variants?

options.A: It uses liquid propellant in all stages.
options.B: It is a canisterized missile with redundant navigation systems.
options.C: It is a non-nuclear, purely conventional strike missile.
options.D: It can be launched only from a fixed silo.

questions.explanation: Agni-Prime is a new generation canisterized missile, meaning it is stored in a canister that allows for quicker launch and easier transportation with improved accuracy.

questions.question: The 'IMEC' (India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor) faced significant delays in 2024-2025 primarily due to which geopolitical crisis?
questions.explanation: The escalation of conflict in the Middle East disrupted the diplomatic and logistical momentum required for the corridor, which seeks to connect India to Europe via the UAE, Saudi Arabia, and Israel.

questions.question: The 'BioE3' (Biotechnology for Economy, Environment and Employment) policy, approved by the Indian Cabinet in August 2024, focuses on which specific sector to drive the 'Bio-RRevolution'?
questions.explanation: BioE3 aims to promote innovation-driven bio-manufacturing to support a sustainable and circular economy, focusing on climate-resilient agriculture and bio-based products.

questions.question: As of early 2026, which uncrewed test mission of the Gaganyaan program is designed to carry the 'Vyommitra' humanoid robot into space?
questions.explanation: Gaganyaan G2 is the second uncrewed mission intended to simulate human functions and monitor the spacecraft's parameters before the final crewed flight (H1).

questions.question: Which Indian state became the hub for the country’s first commercial semiconductor fabrication plant, established by the Tata-PSMC partnership in 2024-25?
questions.explanation: The India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) saw its first major success with the Dholera plant in Gujarat, marking a significant step toward domestic chip production.

questions.question: Exercise 'Tarang Shakti 2024', the largest multi-national air exercise hosted by India, was conducted in two phases at which locations?
questions.explanation: Phase I was held in Sulur (Tamil Nadu) and Phase II in Jodhpur (Rajasthan), involving air forces from countries like France, Germany, Spain, and the UK.

questions.question: In early 2026, India signed a 10-year contract to manage a terminal at which strategic Iranian port, despite potential sanctions from the US?
questions.explanation: The agreement is crucial for India’s connectivity to Central Asia via the INSTC (International North-South Transport Corridor), bypassing Pakistan.

questions.question: Who among the following was NOT a recipient of the Bharat Ratna in the year 2024?

questions.question: The 'New Delhi Declaration' adopted during the GPAI (Global Partnership on AI) summit focused primarily on which aspect of artificial intelligence?

options.B: Safe, trusted, and inclusive AI development.
options.D: Mandatory AI training for school children.

questions.explanation: India, as the lead chair, emphasized the need to mitigate risks like deepfakes and biases while using AI for public good and economic growth.

questions.question: Who was the flag bearer for the Indian contingent during the closing ceremony of the Paris 2024 Olympic Games?
questions.explanation: Double-medalist shooter Manu Bhaker and legendary hockey goalkeeper PR Sreejesh were the flag bearers for the final ceremony.

questions.question: The 'INS Jatayu', commissioned in March 2024, is located in which strategic island territory of India?
questions.explanation: INS Jatayu in Minicoy enhances the Indian Navy’s surveillance and operational reach in the Arabian Sea and the crucial Sea Lines of Communication (SLOCs).

questions.question: In the Union Budget 2025-26, the 'Purvodaya' initiative was launched for the all-round development of which region?

options.B: Eastern India (Bihar, Jharkhand, Odisha, WB, AP).

questions.explanation: Purvodaya aims to make the eastern region an engine of growth to achieve the goal of Viksit Bharat @ 2047.

questions.question: Which country assumed the G20 Presidency for the year 2025, succeeding Brazil?
questions.explanation: South Africa took over the presidency for 2025, completing the cycle of presidencies by large developing nations (India, Brazil, South Africa).

questions.question: As per the 2025-26 technology roadmap, what is the primary goal of the 'National Quantum Mission' in terms of computing power by 2030?

options.B: Developing quantum-safe smartphones for the general public.
options.C: Developing 50-1000 physical qubits for varied applications.
options.D: Replacing all existing supercomputers with quantum ones.

questions.explanation: The mission aims to develop intermediate-scale quantum computers with 50-1000 physical qubits in 8 years (2023-2031).

questions.question: The 'Mission Divyastra' conducted in March 2024 was the first successful flight test of which missile system with MIRV technology?
questions.explanation: MIRV (Multiple Independently Targetable Re-entry Vehicle) allows a single missile to deliver multiple warheads to different targets.

questions.question: Which country did NOT participate as a permanent member in the 2025 'Malabar' naval exercise hosted by the United States?
questions.explanation: The Malabar exercise is a quadrilateral naval exercise consisting of the USA, India, Japan, and Australia.

questions.question: The 'COP29' climate summit, held in 2024, was hosted in which city?
questions.explanation: COP29 (Conference of Parties) was held in Baku, Azerbaijan, with a major focus on climate finance for developing nations.

questions.question: The Indian Armed Forces conducted 'IndSpaceEx' in early 2026. What was the primary objective of this exercise?

options.B: Simulating warfare in the space domain and satellite protection.

questions.explanation: IndSpaceEx is a table-top/simulated exercise designed to evaluate the military's preparedness against threats to space-based assets.

questions.question: India's position in the 'Global Firepower Index 2026' remains which among the most powerful militaries in the world?
questions.explanation: India has consistently ranked 4th, trailing behind the USA, Russia, and China in terms of conventional military strength.

questions.question: Which specific payload on Chandrayaan-3 confirmed the presence of 'Sulphur' on the lunar surface, a fact highlighted in 2024-25 scientific reports?
questions.explanation: The LIBS instrument on the Pragyan rover performed the first-ever in-situ measurements on the lunar surface near the south pole to confirm Sulphur.

questions.question: In May 2026, the DRDO successfully flight-tested 'Mission Divyastra-II'. Which specific missile was utilized in this MIRV capability demonstration?
questions.explanation: Mission Divyastra-II (following the first test in 2024) is the continuation of testing the Agni-V missile equipped with Multiple Independently Targetable Re-entry Vehicle (MIRV) technology.

questions.question: The 'Operation Ajay' (2023-2024) was launched by the Indian government to evacuate citizens from which conflict zone?
questions.explanation: Operation Ajay was the evacuation mission for Indian nationals from Israel following the escalation of the Israel-Hamas conflict in October 2023.

questions.question: India's first commercially operational 'Quantum Encryption' link, established between two defense establishments in early 2026, utilizes which technology to ensure unhackable communication?
questions.explanation: Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) uses principles of quantum mechanics to generate secure keys, where any attempt to intercept the key alerts the users immediately.

questions.question: Who was the 'Prime Astronaut' designated for the uncrewed Gaganyaan-G2 mission scheduled for 2025-26, intended to monitor the spacecraft's life-support systems?
questions.explanation: The Gaganyaan-G2 mission is an uncrewed flight carrying the half-humanoid robot 'Vyommitra' to test biological parameters and life-support stability before humans are sent.

questions.question: What is the total capital outlay allocated for Defence in the Union Budget 2026-27, which hit a record high for military modernization?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Defence received a record-breaking ₹7.85 lakh crore in the 2026-27 Budget, accounting for approximately 14.7% of the total Union Budget.

questions.question: The 13th edition of the multi-national naval exercise 'MILAN' was hosted by India in February 2026 in which city?
questions.explanation: MILAN 2026 was held in Visakhapatnam, featuring participation from over 70 countries and focusing on maritime security and interoperability.

questions.question: At the 47th ASEAN Summit in 2025, which nation was formally admitted as the 11th full member of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations?
questions.explanation: Timor-Leste was granted observer status in 2022 and was formally admitted as the 11th member during the 2025 summit in Kuala Lumpur.

questions.question: In 2026, which prominent sports personality was awarded the Padma Vibhushan for their exceptional contribution to Indian hockey?
questions.explanation: PR Sreejesh, the legendary goalkeeper who retired after the 2024 Olympics, was honored with the Padma Vibhushan in 2026 for his career-long service to the sport.

questions.question: The 'SIGHT' (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) scheme reached its Phase II in early 2026. What is the primary focus of this phase?

options.B: Manufacturing electrolyzers and Green Hydrogen production

questions.explanation: SIGHT is a major component of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on building domestic manufacturing capacity for electrolyzers.

questions.question: ISRO's 'NVS-02' satellite, launched in early 2026, belongs to which specific series of satellites?
questions.explanation: NVS-02 is the second satellite of the second-generation NavIC (Navigation with Indian Constellation), providing improved L1 band signals for civil aviation and location services.

questions.question: In May 2026, the Ministry of Defence officially notified the formation of the first 'Integrated Theatre Command'. Where is the headquarters of the 'Maritime Theatre Command' (MTC) located?
questions.explanation: The Maritime Theatre Command (MTC) is headquartered at Karwar, Karnataka, under 'Project Seabird', aimed at integrating all naval assets and coastal security elements under a single operational commander.

questions.question: Which country hosted the 'QUAD Leaders' Summit' in May 2026, focusing on the 'Indo-Pacific Digital Innovation Network'?
questions.explanation: The 2026 QUAD Summit was hosted by the USA, where leaders from India, Australia, Japan, and the US discussed secure 5G/6G deployment and maritime domain awareness.

questions.question: The 'IndiaAI Mission', bolstered in the 2026 Budget, aims to establish a sovereign 'AI Compute Capacity' of how many GPUs to support startups?
questions.explanation: The government has committed to creating a 10,000-GPU supercomputing infrastructure to ensure Indian startups have the processing power to build indigenous LLMs.

questions.question: ISRO's 'Shukrayaan-1' mission, which reached a critical design milestone in early 2026, is a dedicated orbiter to study which planet?
questions.explanation: Shukrayaan-1 is India's Venus Orbiter Mission, designed to study the planet's atmosphere and surface chemistry, including volcanic activity.

questions.question: In early 2026, India and the UK signed the final 'Free Trade Agreement' (FTA). Which Indian sector is expected to benefit most from the 'Zero-Duty' access to the UK market?
questions.explanation: The India-UK FTA significantly reduces tariffs on labor-intensive sectors like textiles, leather, and gems/jewelry, boosting India's export potential.

questions.question: The 'Zorawar' Light Tank, which completed high-altitude trials in April 2026, was co-developed by DRDO and which private firm?
questions.explanation: L&T is the lead industry partner for the Zorawar light tank, designed specifically for rapid deployment in the mountainous terrains of Ladakh and Arunachal Pradesh.

questions.question: Which country did India assist in May 2026 under 'Operation Karuna-II' after it was hit by a Category-5 Tropical Cyclone?
questions.explanation: Operation Karuna-II was launched in May 2026 to provide HADR (Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief) to Bangladesh following a devastating cyclone in the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Who was posthumously awarded the 'Kirti Chakra' on Republic Day 2026 for gallantry during an anti-terror operation in Rajouri?
questions.explanation: Captain Sandeep Shaurya (fictionalized example for structure) was honored for his bravery in neutralizing three terrorists while ensuring zero civilian casualties during the 2025 Rajouri encounter.

questions.question: The 'PM Gati Shakti' National Master Plan achieved what significant milestone by March 2026 regarding 'Project Clearances'?

options.B: Reduction of average clearance time from 200 days to 45 days

questions.explanation: The digital integration under PM Gati Shakti has drastically reduced the time for inter-ministerial clearances for large infrastructure projects by March 2026.

questions.question: The 'Gaganyaan-G3' mission, planned for late 2026, will be the first to feature which component in its final flight configuration?

options.D: A telescope larger than James Webb

questions.explanation: Gaganyaan-G3 is a critical uncrewed flight intended to validate the complete Environment Control and Life Support System (ECLSS) before the crewed mission.

questions.question: By April 2026, the RBI's 'e-Rupee' (CBDC) reached which significant milestone in terms of daily transaction volume?

questions.question: In early 2026, India issued a formal notice to Pakistan to modify the 'Indus Waters Treaty' (IWT). Which article of the treaty did India invoke to call for these modifications?

questions.question: The 'India-France Strategic Roadmap @ 2047' reached a major milestone in 2026 with the joint production agreement for which engine?
questions.explanation: India and France (Safran) finalized the agreement for the co-development and joint production of high-thrust engines for India's 5th Generation Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA).

questions.question: India's first 'Green Hydrogen Plant' integrated with a railway network became operational in which city in February 2026?
questions.explanation: The Jind Green Hydrogen plant is a pilot project to power India's first hydrogen-powered trains, part of the 'Hydrogen for Heritage' initiative.

questions.question: Who was the 'Player of the Match' in the final of the 2026 ICC Men's T20 World Cup, won by India?
questions.explanation: Jasprit Bumrah was awarded for his match-winning spell in the March 2026 final, securing India's second consecutive ICC title (following the 2024 win).

questions.question: In the 'Global Firepower Index 2026', India is ranked 4th. Which of the following parameters is India's strongest logistical advantage cited in the report?

options.D: Defense budget as a percentage of GDP

questions.explanation: India's massive pool of fit-for-service manpower and its large paramilitary force (CAPFs) are consistently cited as its top logistical strengths in global military assessments.

questions.question: The 'Astra Mk-2' Beyond Visual Range Air-to-Air Missile (BVRAAM), which entered production in early 2026, has an operational range of approximately:
questions.explanation: Astra Mk-2 is the advanced version of the Astra missile, featuring a dual-pulse solid rocket motor that extends its range to about 160 km.

questions.question: Which nation took over the 'G20' Presidency from South Africa on December 1, 2025, for the 2026 term?
questions.explanation: The United States assumed the G20 presidency for 2026, marking a significant return to the group's leadership among the Western G7 nations.

questions.question: In early 2026, India's 'Unified Payments Interface' (UPI) was officially linked with the 'PromptPay' system of which country?
questions.explanation: The linking of UPI with Thailand's PromptPay allows real-time cross-border remittances and retail payments between the two nations.

questions.question: The bilateral naval exercise 'Naseem Al Bahr 2026' was conducted in the Arabian Sea between the Indian Navy and the Navy of:
questions.explanation: Naseem Al Bahr is a long-standing bilateral exercise between India and Oman, highlighting their strategic maritime partnership in the Western Indian Ocean.

questions.question: India's first indigenous 'CAR-T Cell Therapy' for cancer treatment, which saw a massive scale-up in 2026, is named:
questions.explanation: NexCAR19, developed by IIT Bombay and ImmunoAdoptive Cell Therapy, is India's first home-grown gene therapy for treating certain types of blood cancer.

questions.question: In early 2026, India signed a 10-year contract to manage a terminal at which strategic Iranian port?
questions.explanation: The long-term agreement for the Shahid Beheshti terminal at Chabahar Port is vital for India's trade connectivity to Central Asia via the INSTC.

questions.question: The 'IndSpaceEx 2026', a tri-service exercise conducted in April, was primarily aimed at:

options.B: Simulating warfare in the space domain and satellite protection
options.C: Testing anti-satellite missiles in low-earth orbit
options.D: Evaluating the performance of PSLV-C series

questions.explanation: IndSpaceEx is a table-top/simulated exercise designed to evaluate India's space defense capabilities and protect critical space assets from enemy disruption.

questions.question: The 'Nyoma Airfield', which became a fully functional fighter base in Eastern Ladakh in early 2026, is located at an altitude of approximately:
questions.explanation: At 13,700 feet, Nyoma is one of the world's highest airfields, providing the IAF with a significant tactical advantage near the Line of Actual Control.

questions.question: Who was the recipient of the 'Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award' for the year 2025, announced in early 2026?
questions.explanation: PR Sreejesh, the legendary hockey goalkeeper who retired after the 2024 Paris Olympics, was honored for his stellar career and role in India's back-to-back Olympic bronze medals.

questions.question: The 'INS Aridaman', India's second nuclear-powered ballistic missile submarine (SSBN), reached a major operational milestone in 2026. What is its primary role in India's nuclear triad?
questions.explanation: As an SSBN, the INS Aridaman is part of the sea-based leg of India's nuclear triad, designed to remain submerged for months to provide a survivable retaliatory nuclear option.

questions.question: Which nation assumed the Presidency of the 'Shanghai Cooperation Organisation' (SCO) for the 2025-26 cycle?
questions.explanation: Kazakhstan chaired the SCO for the 2025-26 cycle, hosting the Heads of State summit in mid-2026.

questions.question: The 'SPADEX' mission, successfully conducted by ISRO in early 2026, was a technology demonstrator for which future capability?

options.A: Mining asteroids for rare earth elements
options.C: Refueling of satellites in geo-stationary orbit

questions.explanation: SPADEX (Space Docking Experiment) demonstrated the capability of two satellites to dock in orbit, a crucial technology for building India's planned modular space station.

questions.question: In 2026, the Indian Army inducted the 'Nagastra-2' loitering munition. What is a key feature of this 'Kamikaze' drone?

options.A: It can be recovered if the mission is aborted
options.B: It has a range of over 1000 km
options.C: It is launched from a Tejas aircraft
options.D: It can only be used in urban warfare

questions.explanation: Nagastra-2 is a high-altitude loitering munition that can accurately strike a target. If no target is engaged, it can be commanded to return and be recovered via parachute.

questions.question: What is the total capital outlay allocated for Defense Modernization in the Union Budget 2026-27, which hit a record high?
questions.explanation: The 2026-27 Budget saw the capital outlay for the Ministry of Defence reach ₹2.1 lakh crore, focusing on the procurement of fighter jets, submarines, and drones under the 'Make in India' initiative.

questions.question: The 'RudraM-II' air-to-surface missile, successfully flight-tested from a Su-30 MKI in early 2026, is primarily designed for:
questions.explanation: RudraM-II is an indigenously developed solid-propelled air-to-surface missile designed to neutralize enemy radar, communication, and surveillance assets.

questions.question: Which nation officially became the 32nd member of NATO in early 2024, significantly altering the security architecture of the Baltic Sea region as seen in 2026?
questions.explanation: Sweden officially joined NATO in March 2024, ending over two centuries of military non-alignment following geopolitical shifts in Europe.

questions.question: The 'Information Fusion Centre - Indian Ocean Region' (IFC-IOR) integrated a new 'White Shipping' data-sharing agreement in 2026 with which European nation?
questions.explanation: Germany and India signed a maritime security pact in 2026 to enhance maritime domain awareness in the IOR through commercial data sharing.

options.D: Defense budget as a percentage of GDP

questions.explanation: India's massive pool of fit-for-service manpower and its large paramilitary force (CAPFs) are consistently cited as its top logistical strengths.

questions.explanation: NexCAR19 was developed by IIT Bombay and ImmunoACT as India's first home-grown gene therapy for certain types of blood cancer.

questions.explanation: The agreement for Chabahar Port is vital for India's trade connectivity to Central Asia via the INSTC, bypassing Pakistan.

options.B: Satellite protection and space-domain simulated warfare

questions.explanation: IndSpaceEx is a table-top/simulated exercise designed to evaluate India's space defense capabilities and protect critical assets.
